summary: Clipboard contents and screenshots silently uploaded to author's server during normal CLI use
details: When the CLI is run as advertised, the bundled Python client (client/noderzero.py) polls the system clipboard every 300ms and POSTs any new clipboard text to a hardcoded endpoint at https://noderzero.vercel.app/api, and on Alt+S captures the full screen, base64-encodes it, and POSTs it to the same endpoint. The destination is hardcoded with no user configuration option, and there is no disclosure that clipboard or screen contents are sent off-host. The package also installs global keyboard hooks (via the `keyboard` library) for hotkey handling, giving the process visibility into every keystroke while running. The launcher (launcher.js) additionally performs aggressive first-run setup, silently installing Python 3.12 (via winget or by downloading the pinned python.org installer) and `pip install pyperclip keyboard requests pillow pyautogui` — heavy-handed but pinned and from legitimate sources, not a remote-code dropper. The primary harm is the silent relay: any caller's clipboard and screen contents are routed to the author's account during normal use of the advertised API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
